entry.title: Re: Real-World API Calls to S/4HANA (CRUD) from SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
I also initially had problems just now but reloaded and made sure to login. Strangely, without being logged in it gives me the Show API Key and then I get the error you describe. It's strange because I remember it not giving you the button unless you were logged in.

The community is migrating to a new platform this week, and maybe there are some changes in the SAP profile that is causing this problem. I will try to find out more.

I am able to click Login and then get the key. Are you able to log in?

entry.title: Re: SAP Builds Apps using BTP Destination - 500 Internal server error: Unable to fulfill proxy request
entry.content:
Please note that when you create a destination for SAP Build Apps, the URL must point directly to a specific OData service, not to the general system and not to a specific entity. If its not to an OData service, it will return an error and not show data (though I don't remember what error).

This is why I wanted to see what that URL returned from the backend when testing from a different development environment or Postman.

If it's an authentication error, it would likely give you 401 or 403 error.

entry.title: 🇧🇪 SAP Build: Create Apps and Processes Without Coding (Ghent, Belgium)
entry.content:
GOOD NEWS: We have been able to increase our capacity and the maximum number of places is 35. Feel free to continue to sign up.

This is an all new SAP CodeJam event for SAP Build (reworked in February 2024) and will give you a hands-on experience with SAP Build for building apps and business processes without coding (Low Code/No Code). 

You will get to know the environment by creating:

  • A business app (SAP Build Apps).
  • A business process (SAP Build Process Automation).
  • We will also demo desktop automations and a business site in which you can embed the app (SAP Build Work Zone).

You will get to know the SAP Build lobby, where you access all your SAP Build artifacts, and get a deeper understanding of the following topics:

  • SAP Build Apps
    • Connecting to an ABAP (ES5) and CAP service
    • Building UIs
    • Binding data
    • Navigation
    • Triggering processes from your app
    • Scanning barcodes and implementing other mobile capabilities
    • Filtering data
  • SAP Build Process Automation: 
    • Creating a process
    • Adding approvals
    • Implementing a rework scenario
    • Creating actions
    • Calling SAP backends (SAP S/4HANA Cloud and CAP services)
    • Updating data
    • Conditions and GoTo flows
    • Custom variables

This is anin-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Monday, February 19th, 2024 from 09:30 - 17:30 hours CET.The language of the event and the content will be English.

Prerequisites

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser such as Google Chrome or Firefox installed on your laptop.
  • Complete these prerequisites BEFORE you come: Set Up Prerequisites for SAP Build CodeJam 

Location 

Event Address: delaware – Blue Tower 1 (4th floor), Sluisweg 1, 9000 Gent, Belgium.

The instructors are SAP Developer Advocates @Ian_Thain and @thecodester.

The event is kindly being hosted by delaware – thank you! Please note that spaces are limited – we can only accommodate 35 attendees. If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right.

entry.title: 🇩🇪 SAP Build: Create Apps and Processes Without Coding (Herne, Germany)
entry.content:
NOTE: We have reached our maximum registration capacity. If you register at this point you will not be guaranteed a spot but will be put on the waiting list.

For those who could not sign up this time, please wait for the next announcement in this region, or consider hosting your own SAP Build CodeJam.

This is an all new SAP CodeJam event for SAP Build (reworked in February 2024) and will give you a hands-on experience with SAP Build for building apps and business processes without coding (Low Code/No Code). 

You will get to know the environment by creating:

  • A business app (SAP Build Apps).
  • A business process (SAP Build Process Automation).
  • We will also demo desktop automations and a business site in which you can embed the app (SAP Build Work Zone).

You will get to know the SAP Build lobby, where you access all your SAP Build artifacts, and get a deeper understanding of the following topics:

  • SAP Build Apps
    • Connecting to an ABAP (ES5) and CAP service
    • Building UIs
    • Binding data
    • Navigation
    • Triggering processes from your app
    • Scanning barcodes and implementing other mobile capabilities
    • Filtering data
  • SAP Build Process Automation: 
    • Creating a process
    • Adding approvals
    • Implementing a rework scenario
    • Creating actions
    • Calling SAP backends (SAP S/4HANA Cloud and CAP services)
    • Updating data
    • Conditions and GoTo flows
    • Custom variables

This is anin-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Monday, February 21st, 2024 from 09:30 - 17:30 hours CET. The language of the event and the content will be English.

Prerequisites

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Web browser such as Google Chrome or Firefox installed on your laptop.
  • Complete these prerequisites BEFORE you come: Set Up Prerequisites for SAP Build CodeJam 

Location 

Event Address: rku.it GmbH, Berliner Platz 9, Herne, Germany.

The instructors are SAP Developer Advocates @Ian_Thain and @thecodester.

The event is kindly being hosted by rku.it – thank you! Please note that spaces are limited – we can only accommodate 30 attendees. If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right.

entry.title: Re: Is SAP Build Process Automation Desktop Agent for Mac availalbe now?
entry.content:
First, I don’t think it has been announced (I haven’t heard) but check out this video: https://youtu.be/2gB7ipo8TNY?si=XW-yReSterQFMN7r

In the description there is a link to download the agent installer and I now see there are Mac versions, all the way at the bottom:  https://sap.to/6051ulbk9

entry.title: Re: Is SAP Build Process Automation Desktop Agent for Mac availalbe now?
entry.content:
Yes, I’m a Windows person 😺

I forgot to mention that the Mac version does not have all the capabilities of the Windows agent, but I’m traveling and I don’t have the list of capabilities. Maybe someone else can answer what they are … otherwise when I return I will post about the differences.

entry.title: Re: The best Movie Ever!
entry.content: The most fun I had at a movie (excluding dates) is a tie between Strange Brew (“You hosers!”) and Airplane! (“And don’t call me Shirley)

entry.title: Re: What's New for SAP Build Apps - Enhanced Fusion Development
entry.content: This is a real advance ... thanks 😎

entry.title: Re: Build Apps - 500 Error
entry.content: What edition of SAP Build Apps are you using? What landscape?

entry.title: Re: 🇩🇪 SAP Build: Create Apps and Processes Without Coding (Herne, Germany)
entry.content:
Currently, you are on the waiting list, but I will check with the venue to see if there could be additional space, or let you know later this week if a space opens up. 

Thanks for reaching out and your interest, and even if you cannot join, we can arrange for additional events in the area.

Daniel

entry.title: Re: 🇧🇪 SAP Build: Create Apps and Processes Without Coding (Ghent, Belgium)
entry.content:
I have heard of such issues elsewhere, and people were able to delete and reinstall successfully.

If you click the failure button, can you post the error behind it?

entry.title: Re: Build Apps - 500 Error
entry.content: I'll have to test some more. I clicked the link and it also didn't work for me. I then found the app manually in HTML5 Applications in the BTP cockpit, and from there I could open the app.

entry.title: Re: 🇧🇪 SAP Build: Create Apps and Processes Without Coding (Ghent, Belgium)
entry.content: Sorry for the issue ... I'm checking on it.

entry.title: Re: 🇧🇪 SAP Build: Create Apps and Processes Without Coding (Ghent, Belgium)
entry.content:
I installed just now and got that the subscription failed. I deleted the subscription and reran the wizard and it worked for me.

If this doesn't work, please explain precisely how you are installing and uninstalling.

entry.title: Re: 🇧🇪 SAP Build: Create Apps and Processes Without Coding (Ghent, Belgium)
entry.content: Next to subscription failed there should be a "View details" link. Can you send what it says?

entry.title: Re: 🇧🇪 SAP Build: Create Apps and Processes Without Coding (Ghent, Belgium)
entry.content: Can you click "View Details" -- at top, next to red lozenge -- and provide text?

entry.title: Re: 🇧🇪 SAP Build: Create Apps and Processes Without Coding (Ghent, Belgium)
entry.content:
Can anyone who got the failure to unsubscribe message, post here the message they get when clicking "View details".

If you can unsubscribe, please do and reinstall. This worked for me and others when getting this issue.

entry.title: Re: 🇧🇪 SAP Build: Create Apps and Processes Without Coding (Ghent, Belgium)
entry.content: I am still researching this. But if it is possible, can you you delete your trial and create it again. And then, the only difference is when you create the 2 services for SAP Build Process Automation, create the Instance first (standard plan) and let it finish and only then create the subscription (free plan).

entry.title: Re: What's New for SAP Build Apps - Enhanced Fusion Development
entry.content: @EsmeeX I see that there is a new feature of system variables with the current user's details, available on mobile too. Doesn't that mean there is authentication now on mobile?

entry.title: Re: Real-World API Calls to S/4HANA (CRUD) from SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
The property is shown in the video:

https://youtu.be/11TUQgQi-9k?t=115

NOTE: The check connection for destinations is not a real test in many cases, and I assume here it does not send the properties when it tries to check the connection. I believe all it does it check the URL to see if it can be reached, and it can, since 401 indicates the server responded that you need authentication.
